title: HTTP API: POST to exec - cmd not running as array, and erroring as string
body:
### Issue Description

I am attempting to run commands inside my podman containers via the HTTP API. In my test case, I am trying to stop the caddy server within a container named "caddy" using the command "caddy stop" which I can test by trying to access resources it reverse proxies. If I pass a single string as the `Cmd`, I get the following error suggesting it needs to be an array:

>  "cause": "json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field ExecCreateConfig.Cmd of type []string",
    "message": "decoding request body as JSON: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field ExecCreateConfig.Cmd of type []string",
    "response": 500

This is strange, as the api docs state you can use a single string or an array.

If I replace the single string with an array containing only one string (`["caddy stop"]`), I get an Id as a response, but the command seemingly did not execute.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start a container called `caddy`
1. Start the podman API with `podman system service -t 0 tcp:ipaddress:port`
2. POST to http://`ipaddress`:`port`/v4.5.0/libpod/containers/caddy/exec with the following body:
```
{
    "Cmd": ["caddy stop"]
}
```

### Describe the results you received

Got a response with an Id, but command does not appear to have been executed in container.

### Describe the results you expected

Command should have been executed in the container (stopping the caddy service, in this instance).

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running podman on Fedora Server on a physical server.

### Additional information

I have also tried including 
```
    "User": "root",
    "Privileged": true
```
in the POST request body, with the same results.

I have also tried separating the Cmd in the array per someone's suggestion `["caddy", "stop"]` and still get the same result.

comments.body: You have to start the exec session with the ID that was returned, https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/exec/operation/ExecStartLibpod

That did it, thank you! For reference, to anyone else who may stumble upon this, I did have to split the command into an array, so I used `"Cmd": ["caddy","stop"]` in the request body.

I'm not sure if this process of creating an exec session and then starting it with a separate request is common practice, but I feel like the documentation could use an additional comment about this. Even just a single sentence in the API docs in the exec section.

Feel free to close this if you want to track documentation change requests elsewhere (or if you believe it isn't needed) - my issue is resolved.
